name: National Rifle Association (NRA)
mission: Opposes gun control legislation and promotes firearms ownership rights under the 2nd Amendment. Lobbies against universal background checks, red flag laws, assault weapons bans, and magazine capacity limits.

name: PhRMA - Pharmaceutical Research & Manufacturers
mission: The largest single-sector lobbying spender in Washington most years. Opposes Medicare drug price negotiation, generic drug competition, and drug importation. Supports extended patent protections to maintain monopoly pricing.

name: American Petroleum Institute (API)
mission: Represents oil, gas, and natural gas industries. Spends hundreds of millions opposing climate legislation and carbon taxes. Promotes fossil fuel subsidies, pipeline expansion, and rollback of EPA regulations.

name: U.S. Chamber of Commerce
mission: Largest lobbying organization in the US by total spend. Opposes minimum wage increases, labor union rights, environmental regulations, and corporate tax increases.

name: AIPAC - American Israel Public Affairs Committee
mission: Promotes unconditional U.S. military and financial aid to Israel. AIPAC's Super PAC spent over $100M in the 2022 election cycle - more than any other outside group.
keyPositions: Oppose any arms embargo on Israel
Support Iron Dome and missile defense funding

name: Wall Street / Financial Industry
mission: Lobbies for financial deregulation, against consumer protection measures, and for lower taxes on capital gains and investment income.

name: Defense Industry (Military-Industrial Complex)
mission: Top contractors Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Boeing, Northrop Grumman, and General Dynamics collectively spend $150M+ annually lobbying for defense budget increases and weapons procurement contracts.

name: AFL-CIO / Labor Unions
mission: Supports union organizing rights, minimum wage increases, worker protections, and collective bargaining. Opposes right-to-work laws and anti-union legislation.

name: National Education Association (NEA)
mission: Largest US labor union representing 3 million teachers. Lobbies for education funding, teacher rights, and opposes school vouchers and for-profit education.

name: Big Tech Industry
mission: Represents major technology and platform companies. Lobbies on antitrust, data privacy, content moderation (Section 230), AI regulation, and H-1B visa policy. Among the fastest-growing lobbying sectors in Washington.
keyPositions: Limit antitrust breakups of large platforms

name: Real Estate Industry (NAR)
mission: Led by the National Association of Realtors, one of the largest lobbying spenders. Protects the mortgage interest deduction, opposes commission-structure reforms, and shapes housing finance and zoning policy.
keyPositions: Oppose limits on real estate commissions

name: Health Insurance Industry (AHIP)
mission: Represents health insurers through AHIP and member companies. Opposes a public option and single-payer, shapes Affordable Care Act rules, and influences Medicare Advantage payment policy.

name: Agribusiness (Farm Bureau)
mission: Represents large agricultural producers and the Farm Bureau. Shapes the Farm Bill, crop subsidies and insurance, trade policy, and opposes some environmental and labor regulations affecting farms.
keyPositions: Maximize farm subsidies and crop insurance
Influence farm labor and immigration policy

name: Telecommunications Industry
mission: Represents telecom and media carriers. Lobbies on net neutrality, spectrum allocation, broadband subsidies, and media ownership rules.

name: Cryptocurrency Industry
mission: Represents digital asset firms and exchanges. A rapidly growing political spender, including through Super PACs. Seeks favorable regulatory classification and opposes strict SEC oversight of tokens.
keyPositions: Limit SEC enforcement over digital assets
Back crypto-friendly candidates via Super PACs

name: Insurance Industry
mission: Represents property, casualty, and life insurers. Shapes state and federal insurance regulation, opposes federal oversight expansion, and influences disaster and liability policy.
keyPositions: Shape disaster and flood insurance policy

name: Electric Utilities & Energy
mission: Represents electric utilities and power generators. Shapes grid policy, rate structures, renewable mandates, and emissions regulation.
keyPositions: Shape electricity rate and grid policy

name: Transportation & Airlines
mission: Represents airlines, railroads, and shipping. Lobbies on infrastructure funding, safety regulation, labor rules, and consumer protection requirements.
keyPositions: Shape airline and rail safety rules
Support fuel and emissions standards favorable to industry

name: Lawyers & Law Firms
mission: Represents trial lawyers, bar associations, and large law firms. Opposes tort reform and liability caps, and shapes civil justice and class-action policy.
keyPositions: Oppose tort reform and damage caps
Protect class-action and civil litigation rights

name: Retail & Consumer Goods
mission: Represents retailers and consumer-goods makers. Lobbies on trade and tariffs, labor and minimum-wage rules, swipe-fee policy, and product regulation.
keyPositions: Shape product safety and labeling rules

name: Construction & Building Trades
mission: Represents construction firms and building trades. Shapes infrastructure spending, prevailing-wage rules, immigration and labor policy, and building and safety codes.
keyPositions: Influence construction labor and immigration rules
Limit costly safety and code mandates

name: Leadership PACs & Party Committees
mission: Captures money flowing through leadership PACs and party committees rather than a single industry. These vehicles let lawmakers and parties pool and redistribute donor funds to allies.
keyPositions: Pool and redistribute donor funds to allies

name: Ideological / Single-Issue Groups
mission: Catch-all for ideological and single-issue advocacy groups across the spectrum (abortion, environment, civil liberties, and others) that give based on cause rather than industry profit.
keyPositions: Advance a specific cause or ideology
Fund aligned candidates regardless of party
Score and rate lawmakers on the issue

name: Other PAC & Corporate Money
mission: Catch-all bucket for organized PAC and corporate contributions that do not map to a named sector above. Surfaced so no classified donor money is left invisible.
keyPositions: Ensure all organized money is accounted for
Flag donors for future sector mapping
Maintain completeness of the funding picture
